title: C++ Default Parameters
content:
### Default Parameters

Default parameters allow function arguments to have default values. If the caller omits the argument, the default value is used.

---

### Syntax

```cpp
returnType functionName(type param = default_value);
```

---

### Example

```cpp
void greet(string name = "Guest") {
  cout << "Hello, " << name;
}

greet();        // Hello, Guest
greet("John");  // Hello, John
```

---

### Multiple Default Parameters

```cpp
void display(int x, int y = 10, string label = "Point") {
  cout << label << ": (" << x << ", " << y << ")";
}

display(5);            // Point: (5, 10)
display(5, 20);        // Point: (5, 20)
display(5, 20, "Pos");  // Pos: (5, 20)
```

---

### Rules

| Rule | Explanation |
|------|-------------|
| Right to left | Defaults must go from rightmost parameter |
| Declaration only | Specify default only in declaration, not definition |
| Function overloading | Can conflict with overloaded functions |

---

### Incorrect Usage

```cpp
// ERROR: default parameter not at the end
void bad(int x = 5, int y);

// CORRECT
void good(int x, int y = 5);
```

---

> **Key Point:** Default parameters must be the rightmost parameters. Once a parameter has a default, all subsequent parameters must also have defaults.

quiz.question: Where should default parameters be placed?
